case.title: BOLTON v. BOROUGH OF SCHUYLKILL HAVEN

opinion.h: BOLTON v. BOROUGH OF SCHUYLKILL HAVEN, 365 U.S. 767 (1961)
BOLTON ET AL. v. BOROUGH OF SCHUYLKILL HAVEN. APPEAL FROM THE SUPERIOR COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA. No. 691. Decided April 17, 1961.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h: WALGREEN CO. v. COMMISSIONER OF TAXATION, 365 U.S. 767 (1961)
opinion.p: 365 U.S. 767 WALGREEN CO. v. COMMISSIONER OF TAXATION. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF MINNESOTA. No. 732. Decided April 17, 1961.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Walter F. Mondale, Attorney General of Minnesota, Perry Voldness, Assistant Attorney General, and Jerome J. Sicora, Special Assistant Attorney General, for respondent.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
